included_models: MLP, DNN, DBN, DeepNet, IDSNet, AlertNet, FS-Net, MaMPF, other fully-connected custom NN
notes: Generic dense / fully-connected networks used for tabular data or IDS-specific custom networks

included_models: CNN, including 1-D/2-D CNN for traffic features
notes: Dedicated to spatial/feature-map learning, often used for image-like flow representations

included_models: RNN, LSTM, GRU, C-LSTM (CNN+LSTM), Transformer, Bi-LSTM, sequential encoders
notes: Capture temporal or sequential dependencies in network flows or event logs

included_models: AE, sparse/denoising variants, Kitsune/KitNet, FS-Net (when AE-like), hybrid AE components
notes: Used for reconstruction-error anomaly detection or feature compression

included_models: GAN, WGAN, AdvGAN, EVAGAN, diffusion-based generative models
notes: Used for adversarial example generation, data purification, or synthetic traffic creation

included_models: GNN, GraphSAGE, GAT, other graph-structured neural models
notes: Capture relational dependencies between flows, hosts, or features in network graphs
Domain-tailored IDS architectures, usually extending fully-connected or AE paradigms

included_models: DT, RF, ExtraTrees, GBDT, XGB, LGBM, AdaBoost, Diff-RF
Logistic Regression, Naïve Bayes, OneR, Bayesian Network, SVM

notes: Distance-based supervised method, used as baseline or for hybrid ensembles

included_models: Explicit combination of two or more model families (e.g., RF + XGB + MLP, voting/stacking/bagging)
notes: Used when multiple classifiers are jointly deployed or fused

included_models: IFOR, RRCF (Robust Random Cut Forest), LOF (Local Outlier Factor), OCSVM, SOM
notes: Unsupervised anomaly detection methods for distributional outlier identification
